WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

Off Calendar Mar-18-2020

Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Produce Further Responses To Medical Records Subpoena To Palo Alto Medical Foundation, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,512, And Supporting Memorandum
Off Calendar Jan-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ross Psyhogios, Esq. 415-431-5310 of Jones Clifford for Plaintiff.
Andrew Johnson, Esq. 310-557-2030 of Condon & Forsyth LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues providing an amneded privilege log. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MENZIES AVIATION (U.S.A) INC., ANDREW DODGE's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Produce Further Responses To Medical Records Subpoena To Palo Alto Medical Foundation, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,512.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem are adopted. Defendants' motion to compel Plaintiff to produce further responses to medical records subpoena to Palo Alto Medical Foundation is denied in part for reasons stated on the record. Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Defendant Dodges Attendance And Testimony At Deposition
Off Calendar Oct-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS ROBERT BIERNAT, CATHERINE BIERNAT'S Motion To Compel Defendant Dodges Attendance And Testimony At Deposition

The Motion appears to be unopposed. The Motion is granted. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and send by email to Judge Pro Tem Julia Campins at julia@campinsbenhambaker.com. Judge Pro Tem: Julia Campins; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Menzies Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Oct-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ross J. Psyhogios, Esq. of Jones Clifford, T:888.625.2251, for plaintiff/moving party;
Andrew C. Johnson, Esq. of Condon & Forsyth, LLP, T:310.557.2030, for defendant/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ROBERT BIERNAT, CATHERINE BIERNAT's Motion To Compel Defendant Menzies Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion for an order compelling Defendant Menzies Aviation (USA), Inc. to provide further responses to requests for production Nos. 6-8 of Plaintiff's second set of requests for production of documents, and to produce documents responsive to those requests, is granted.

Defendants to produce responses and documents within 30 days.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
